description: Figure 9 presents experimental validation of the Fe-30Mn-8Al-1C low-density steel processed at 1000°C/15 min and aged at 550°C/2 h. Panel (a) shows element distribution maps for Fe, Mn, Al, and C alongside a secondary electron image (SEI) and a backscattered electron composition image (COMPO), all at a 20 μm scale; the uniformly colored elemental maps visually indicate homogeneous elemental distribution without significant segregation, supporting the formation of a single-phase austenitic matrix. Panel (b) displays the engineering stress–strain curve for the same alloy; caption reports a yield strength of 953 MPa, an ultimate tensile strength of 1109 MPa, and an elongation of 42%. Panel (c) provides an optical micrograph at a 50 μm scale showing a fully recrystallized, equiaxed grain structure. Collectively, these results confirm that the machine-learning-designed composition achieves the predicted uniform single-phase microstructure coupled with excellent strength–ductility synergy.
